data.title: The future of auto software: Could Rivian be the Android to Tesla's Apple status?
data.description:
A recent article in FT speculated on the potential for Rivian Automotive (RIVN) to license its  electrical architecture and autonomous software. "If you don’t have a high level of autonomy and an increasingly software- and AI-defined vehicle, you’ll lose market share," stated Rivian (RIVN) CEO RJ Scaringe on the future of the auto industry.

UBS analyst Joseph Spak said the idea that the EVs from legacy OEMs could benefit from a common software platform does make sense since it could reduce the cost to make EVs and lower the capital intensity. He said under that scenario vehicles become more homogenized/commoditized from a hardware and base software perspective. "This would then become a bigger test of each individual company’s brand strength to command a premium and/or company scale to generate a profit," he noted. 

Looking ahead, Spak and his team see the potential for an automotive industry that looks more like the smartphone landscape, which is supported by large platforms. That could evolve to Tesla (TSLA) taking on the Apple (AAPL) role and Rivian (RIVN) filling the gap in the Android role.

That leads to the questions of whether Rivian's (RIVN) desire to sell their vehicles becomes a hindrance to the idea of becoming the base architecture/software platform, the role of the Volkswagen (VLKAF) partnership in terms of Rivian (RIVN) licensing its AV software, and the openness of legacy OEMs to enter into AV platform partnerships.

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) were up 1.2% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

data.title: MSCI declares $2.05 dividend
data.description:
• MSCI (MSCI) declares $2.05/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.45%
  • Payable May 29; for shareholders of record May 15; ex-div May 15.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $2.05 for two consecutive quarters.
  • See MSCI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Northern Trust's Q1 earnings exceed estimates on improved credit quality, revenue lags
data.description:
Northern Trust's (NTRS) first-quarter earnings surpassed expectations as provisions surprised to the upside, given an improved credit quality at the commercial and institutional portfolio.

The company reported Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.71, which beats the average analyst estimate by $0.38, and revenue of $2.21B (+13.3% Y/Y) that misses consensus by $60M.

Trust, investment, and other servicing fees increased 11% year-over-year to $1.34B. Other noninterest income rose 33% to $210.2M. Net interest income was up 15% to $661.6M.

Comparatively, the S&P Cap IQ estimate for net interest income stands at $630.8M.

Provision for credit losses was recorded at a negative $3.0M, compared to the prior year's positive $1.0M. The S&P Cap IQ estimate for Q1 loan loss provision stands at a positive $5.08M.

The negative provision in the current quarter resulted from a decrease in the collective reserve, primarily driven by improved credit quality of the commercial and institutional portfolio.

"Revenue increased 14% year-over-year, supported by improved organic growth, elevated client activity across our businesses, and favorable interest rate and market conditions," said CEO Michael O'Grady.

"Disciplined expense management and a sustained focus on operational efficiency generated more than 700 basis points of operating leverage, a 32% pre-tax margin, and a 43% rise in earnings per share. Return on equity reached 17.4%, and we returned 100% of our earnings to shareholders," said O'Grady.

Earnings conference call is scheduled for 8:00 AM CT today.

data.title: Managed care stocks jump after UNH's Q1 report: Here are the 9 best Quant rated names
data.description:
Managed care stocks got a broad lift in Tuesday's premarket session after UnitedHealth Group (UNH), the sector's bellwether and the first major insurer to report this earnings season, posted better-than-expected Q1 2026 results and raised its full-year earnings outlook. 

UNH surged approximately 7% on the news, with the positive read-through lifting peers across the board: Humana (HUM), CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), Clover Health (CLOV), and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC) all traded higher

However, UnitedHealth ranks only fourth on Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system. With a Hold rating of 3.37, UNH trails smaller peers Centene Corporation (CNC), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), and HealthEquity (HQY) in the rankings, even though it carries by far the largest market cap on the list at $293.6 billion.

Topping the rankings is Centene (CNC) with a Strong Buy rating of 4.67, followed by Alignment Healthcare (ALHC) at 4.30 and HealthEquity (HQY) at 3.60, the only three names to clear the bullish 3.5 threshold. The bottom half of the list presents a more cautious picture, with Elevance Health (ELV), Humana (HUM), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Clover Health (CLOV) all carrying Hold scores.

Here is the list:

  1. Centene Corporation (CNC), Quant Rating: 4.67

  2. Alignment Healthcare, Inc. (ALHC), Quant Rating: 4.30

  3. HealthEquity, Inc. (HQY), Quant Rating: 3.60

  4. UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH), Quant Rating: 3.37

  5. Progyny, Inc. (PGNY), Quant Rating: 3.14

  6. Elevance Health, Inc. (ELV), Quant Rating: 2.99

  7. Humana Inc. (HUM), Quant Rating: 2.81

  8. Molina Healthcare, Inc. (MOH), Quant Rating: 2.64

  9. Clover Health Investments, Corp. (CLOV), Quant Rating: 2.59

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH)

data.title: Laird Superfood falls 15% after $48M Terrasoul deal, $60M funding
data.description:
• Laird Superfood (LSF) announced an acquisition of Terrasoul Superfoods for $48M in cash, subject to customary purchase price adjustments, with an additional earnout of up to $5M payable in cash if certain performance-based milestones are achieved. 
  • The deal was funded through a concurrent $60M private placement of Series A convertible preferred stock to affiliates of Nexus Capital Management, which will take its ownership to about 71.7% of the company on a fully diluted basis.
  • Terrasoul, a vertically integrated superfoods platform, generated ~$65.8M in unaudited net sales in 2025.
  • LSF (LSF) stock price dipped about 15% on Tuesday during pre-market trade.

data.title: Northrop Grumman's shares slip as outlook matches estimates
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) beat Wall Street estimates for the first quarter but shares fell about 1% in premarket trading Tuesday as investors focused on full-year guidance that largely matched revenue expectations while trailing profit forecasts.

 

 

Northrop Grumman (NOC) reported first-quarter revenue of $9.88 billion, above analysts’ consensus estimate of $9.76 billion. Earnings of $6.14 a share also topped expectations of $6.06.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) reaffirmed its 2026 forecast for sales of $43.5 billion to $44.0 billion, compared with the consensus estimate of $43.9 billion, and MTM-adjusted earnings of $27.40 to $27.90 a share, below the consensus forecast of $27.94. It also maintained its free cash flow outlook of $3.1 billion to $3.5 billion.

The muted guidance appeared to outweigh the quarterly beat, underscoring how investors in defense stocks are increasingly focused on forward estimates, margin expansion and cash generation rather than backward-looking results alone.

Profit surges on higher sales, easier comps

Net income rose to $875 million, or $6.14 a share, from $481 million, or $3.32 a share.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) said operating income climbed 73% to $989 million, while operating margin improved to 10.0% from 6.1% a year earlier. The gains were helped in part by the absence of a prior-year B-21 loss provision that weighed on results in early 2025.

CEO highlights bookings, production strength

“Northrop Grumman delivered strong first quarter results, with continued robust bookings, mid-single-digit organic sales growth, and solid operating performance,” Chair, Chief Executive Officer and President Kathy Warden said in the earnings release.

Warden added that the company’s manufacturing capacity and portfolio position it to deliver technology “at speed and scale” for customers.

B-21, Sentinel programs drive momentum

Northrop Grumman (NOC) said first-quarter net awards totaled $9.8 billion, leaving backlog at $95.6 billion. During the quarter, the company reached agreements with the U.S. Air Force to expand B-21 production capacity and accelerate Sentinel initial operating capability.

The B-21 Raider is Northrop Grumman’s (NOC) next-generation stealth bomber designed for long-range strike missions, while Sentinel is the U.S. Air Force’s new land-based intercontinental ballistic missile system intended to replace the aging Minuteman III nuclear deterrent.

Revenue from aeronautics systems rose 17% to $3.28 billion, helped by higher sales on the B-21 and other restricted programs. Defense systems sales increased 5% to $1.90 billion as the Sentinel missile modernization program ramped.

data.title: AXT slides after $550M stock offering priced at a discount
data.description:
Shares of AXT (AXTI) fell nearly 12% at $69 in premarket trading on Tuesday after the semiconductor materials maker priced a $550M worth public offering of common stock at a discount.

The company said it will sell 8,560,311 shares at $64.25 each, making proceeds of about $550M before fees and expenses. AXT's closing price on Monday was $78.76.

AXT also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,284,046 shares. If fully exercised, total gross proceeds would rise to about $632.5M.The company said it plans to use the proceeds primarily to support subsidiary Beijing Tongmei Xtal Technology as it expands capacity for indium phosphide substrate production for export markets and for research and development, working capital, and other corporate purposes.

Northland is acting as sole bookrunner on the deal, with Needham, B. Riley, Craig-Hallum Capital, and Wedbush Securities serving as co-managers.The offering is expected to close on April 22.

data.title: Core Scientific subsidiary plans $3.3B notes offering
data.description:
• Core Scientific (CORZ) wholly-owned subsidiary, Core Scientific Finance I LLC, plans to offer $3.3B of senior secured notes due 2031 in a private offering.
  • The issuer plans to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund a debt service reserve account, and the remaining proceeds to make a distribution to Core Scientific.
  • Core Scientific (CORZ) intends to use a portion of the net proceeds it receives to repay in full its outstanding delayed draw term loans under its previously announced 364-day credit facility, including accrued interest thereon and fees and expenses in connection therewith.
  • CORZ shares rose +4.6% premarket.

data.title: Soluna expands Blockware partnership with 3.3 MW addition at West Texas site, shares jump
data.description:
Soluna Holdings (SLNH) Tuesday announced a new expansion of its partnership with Blockware. The agreement adds 3.3 MW of capacity at Soluna’s Project Dorothy 1B site in West Texas. This marks Blockware’s fourth expansion with Soluna and its first deployment at Project Dorothy 1B.

Soluna Holdings (SLNH) surged 16% in premarket trading.

With this latest addition, Blockware becomes the first customer signed at Dorothy 1B, a 25 MW data center co-located with and powered by the Briscoe Wind Farm. Soluna (SLNH) recently acquired the Briscoe wind farm in a $53M transaction, enabling full vertical integration between its energy generation and data center operations. 

Across all sites, Blockware’s total deployed capacity with Soluna now exceeds 17 MW.

“As Blockware’s capacity needs continue to grow, Soluna has consistently delivered,” said Mason Jappa, CEO of Blockware. “Their ability to launch new sites allows us to deepen our partnership and scale alongside them.”

data.title: 3M in charts: Q1 revenue grew Y/Y across all business segments
